subject: Social Science (Geography)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the importance of agriculture in India
Identify different types of farming practiced in India
Learn about cropping seasons and cropping patterns
Identify major food and cash crops grown in India
Understand plantation agriculture and its importance
Learn about technological and institutional reforms in agriculture
Develop map skills to identify major crop-producing regions

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Agriculture
topics_covered: Agricultural products used in daily life
teaching_strategy: Use real-life examples like rice, wheat, and vegetables
student_activities: List agricultural products used at home
Discuss why agriculture is important for India

lesson_plan.title: Types of Farming
student_activities: Create a comparison table of farming types
Identify examples of each farming system

lesson_plan.title: Cropping Seasons and Cropping Patterns
student_activities: List crops grown in each season

lesson_plan.title: Major Crops of India
student_activities: Identify crop-producing states on a map
Match crops with their growing conditions

lesson_plan.title: Food Crops and Cash Crops
student_activities: List food crops and non-food crops
Discuss how crops are used in industries

lesson_plan.title: Fibre Crops and Plantation Agriculture
student_activities: Identify fibre crops and their uses
Create a chart showing plantation crops

lesson_plan.title: Agricultural Reforms and Revision
student_activities: Write two reforms that improved agriculture

teacher_tip: Use maps and real-life examples of crops grown in different regions to help students visualize agricultural diversity in India.
